practice: Limit system access to authorized users; processes acting on behalf of authorized users; and devices (including other systems)
notes: Per-tool RBAC via assertPermission(); all tool invocations gated against OrgMember role bits before execution

practice: Limit system access to the types of transactions and functions that authorized users are permitted to execute
notes: 42 executors each declare required permission bit; cosmos_app DB role revoked UPDATE/DELETE on audit tables; least-privilege DB role documented in migration 20260606050000

practice: Control the flow of CUI in accordance with approved authorizations
notes: Single fail-closed egress path in src/lib/ai/egress/; MAC ceiling (FOUO+) withheld regardless of tenant class; EgressDecision logged per turn; arch test enforces single-path

practice: Separate the duties of individuals to reduce the risk of malevolent activity without collusion
notes: OrgMember permission bits separate read/write/admin roles; platform-owner vs tenant-admin separation designed; full duty-separation matrix and SoD policy doc not yet authored

practice: Employ the principle of least privilege including for specific security functions and privileged accounts
notes: cosmos_app DB role has INSERT/SELECT/UPDATE/DELETE on app tables; REVOKE UPDATE/DELETE/TRUNCATE on audit_logs and egress_decisions; root DB role reserved for migrations only

practice: Use non-privileged accounts or roles when accessing non-security functions
notes: App runs as cosmos_app (non-superuser); tools execute as authenticated org member; no privilege escalation path in executor chain

practice: Prevent non-privileged users from executing privileged functions and capture the execution of such functions in audit logs
notes: assertPermission() throws on insufficient bits; all tool executions logged to audit_logs; egress chokepoint logs EgressDecision for every model-bound value

practice: Limit unsuccessful logon attempts
notes: Session invalidation on auth failure exists; no explicit configurable lockout counter or account lockout policy implemented yet; planned for SSO/SAML phase

practice: Provide privacy and security notices consistent with CUI rules
notes: System banner / privacy notice text is a policy artifact; SSP narrative references the requirement; UI banner hook exists but content not yet authored

practice: Use session lock with pattern-hiding displays after a period of inactivity
notes: Session expires via expires_at column; client-side idle lock not yet implemented; planned

practice: Terminate (automatically) a user session after a defined condition
notes: Sessions store expires_at; middleware rejects expired sessions; gov tenants can configure shorter session duration via security settings API

practice: Monitor and control remote access sessions
notes: Audit log captures remote actions; dedicated remote-access session monitoring dashboard not yet built; planned for observability phase

practice: Employ cryptographic mechanisms to protect the confidentiality of remote access sessions
notes: TLS terminated by Caddy reverse proxy; all HTTP traffic to cosmos service is internal-network only; X-Forwarded-Proto enforced

practice: Route remote access via managed access control points
notes: Single ingress via Caddy reverse proxy; direct port exposure disabled in compose; cosmos service not on public network

practice: Authorize remote execution of privileged commands and access to security-relevant information via remote access only for documented operational needs
notes: Requires written operational procedures documenting approved remote-admin scenarios; policy not yet authored

practice: Authorize wireless access prior to allowing such connections
notes: Infrastructure/facility wireless policy; out of software scope; policy required by deploying organization

practice: Protect wireless access using authentication and encryption
notes: Infrastructure/facility wireless policy; out of software scope; policy required by deploying organization

practice: Control connection of mobile devices
notes: MDM/mobile device policy; out of software scope; policy required by deploying organization

practice: Encrypt CUI on mobile devices and mobile computing platforms
notes: Device encryption policy for endpoints; out of software scope; policy required by deploying organization

practice: Verify and control/limit connections to external systems
notes: SSRF guard on outbound webhook URLs; external connector allowlist enforced; gov tenants code-blocked from Nango/external MCP sidecars

practice: Limit use of portable storage devices on external systems
notes: Physical/endpoint policy; out of software scope; policy required by deploying organization

practice: Control CUI posted or processed on publicly accessible systems
notes: Egress chokepoint withholds CUI/FOUO from model (external system); public-facing API routes require authentication; no anonymous CUI access paths

practice: Ensure that managers; supervisors; and security personnel are aware of the security risks associated with their activities and of the applicable policies; standards; and procedures related to the security of organizational systems
notes: Security awareness training program; policy and training content required by deploying organization

practice: Ensure that organizational personnel are trained to carry out their assigned information security responsibilities
notes: Role-based security training program; policy and training content required by deploying organization

practice: Provide security awareness training on recognizing and reporting potential threats
notes: Threat recognition training (phishing/social engineering); policy and training content required by deploying organization

practice: Create and retain system audit logs and records to the extent needed to enable the monitoring; analysis; investigation; and reporting of unlawful or unauthorized system activity
notes: append-only audit_logs table (trigger-enforced); append-only egress_decisions; gov 3yr retention floor enforced via GOV_AUDIT_RETENTION_FLOOR_DAYS=1095 in security settings API

practice: Ensure that the actions of individual users can be traced to those users so they can be held accountable for their actions
notes: audit_logs records userId; orgId; action; entity; entityId; ipAddress; all tool calls logged via tool-executor.ts with acting userId; FK-decoupled (org delete no longer cascades to logs)

practice: Review and process audit logs in a timely manner to detect inappropriate or unusual activity
notes: Audit log data is stored and queryable; automated alerting/SIEM integration and formal review cadence not yet implemented; planned for observability phase

practice: Alert in the event of an audit logging process failure
notes: DB-level trigger failures surface as errors; application-level alert on audit-write failure not yet wired to an alerting sink; planned for observability phase

practice: Correlate audit record review; analysis; and reporting processes for investigation and response to indications of unlawful; unauthorized; suspicious; or unusual activity
notes: Logs are append-only and queryable; cross-event correlation tooling (SIEM/log aggregation) not yet deployed; planned for observability phase

practice: Provide audit record reduction and report generation to support on-demand analysis and after-the-fact investigation
notes: Audit log table supports query by orgId/userId/action/time; formal report-generation tooling not yet built; planned

practice: Provide a system capability that compares and synchronizes internal system clocks with an authoritative source to generate time stamps for audit records
notes: PostgreSQL CURRENT_TIMESTAMP used for audit timestamps; server time synced via NTP in container image; createdAt IDENTITY seq provides monotonic ordering

practice: Protect audit information and audit tools from unauthorized access; modification; and deletion
notes: DB trigger blocks UPDATE/DELETE on audit_logs and egress_decisions; REVOKE UPDATE/DELETE/TRUNCATE from cosmos_app role; only postgres superuser retains schema-modification rights

practice: Limit management of audit logging to a subset of privileged users
notes: Audit retention settings gated behind platform-owner role; cosmos_app cannot modify audit table schema; retention config requires SECURITY_SETTINGS_MANAGE permission bit

practice: Establish and maintain baseline configurations and inventories of organizational systems
notes: Syft SBOM (SPDX-JSON) enumerates all packages in the container image; published to GHCR as attestation; security.yml gate generates per-build SBOM

practice: Establish and maintain secure configuration settings for information technology products employed in organizational systems
notes: hadolint scans Dockerfile for configuration weaknesses; Checkov scans compose manifests; non-root container user enforced; E2E_TEST_AUTH asserted absent in prod build

practice: Track; review; approve; and log changes to organizational systems
notes: Git history + CI gates track code changes; formal change-management board process and approval workflow not yet documented; planned as policy artifact

practice: Analyze the security impact of changes prior to implementation
notes: CodeQL SAST + Trivy SCA gate on every PR provides automated security analysis; manual security-impact review process not yet formalized; partial credit

practice: Define; document; approve; and enforce physical and logical access restrictions associated with changes to organizational systems
notes: Change-management access policy; branch protection and required-reviewer settings are controls but formal policy not yet authored

practice: Employ the principle of least functionality by configuring organizational systems to provide only essential capabilities
notes: Multi-stage Dockerfile strips build tools from runtime image; non-root user; minimal OS packages; E2E test route compile-excluded from production image

practice: Restrict; disable; or prevent the use of nonessential programs; functions; ports; protocols; and services
notes: Only port 3000 (cosmos) exposed internally; external ports only via Caddy reverse proxy; no SSH daemon in container; --mcp-config absent from gov image by code invariant

practice: Apply deny-by-default and allow-by-exception policy to prevent the use of unauthorized software
notes: Gov tenants have default-deny classification policy; Nango/external-MCP code-blocked for gov; formal application allowlist/whitelisting process not yet documented

practice: Control and monitor user-installed software
notes: End-user software installation policy; out of software scope for the SaaS application itself; policy required by deploying organization

practice: Identify system users; processes acting on behalf of users; and devices
notes: Users authenticated via session token; userId tracked on all audit records; orgId scopes every data query; device/process identity tracked via ipAddress on audit entries

practice: Authenticate (or verify) the identities of those users; processes; or devices as a prerequisite to allowing access to organizational systems
notes: Session middleware rejects unauthenticated requests; all API routes require valid session; session cookie signed and expires_at enforced

practice: Use multifactor authentication for local and network access to privileged accounts and for network access to non-privileged accounts
notes: Current auth is single-factor (Google OAuth or password); MFA/phishing-resistant second factor planned for SSO/SAML phase; IA-2(1/2) 3.5.3 is a gov-go-live gate dependency

practice: Employ replay-resistant authentication mechanisms
notes: Session tokens are single-use at creation; CSRF protection via SameSite cookie; replay-resistant MFA (FIDO2/WebAuthn) planned for SSO phase

practice: Employ identifier management
notes: User and org UUIDs are system-generated; no reuse; session IDs are random UUIDs; ApiKey uses prefix+hash pattern (no raw secret storage)

practice: Disable identifiers after a defined inactivity period
notes: Sessions expire via expires_at; inactive user account lockout / identifier disable after configurable inactivity not yet implemented; planned

practice: Enforce a minimum password complexity and change requirements
notes: Password auth path defers to Google OAuth (password policy enforced by Google); local password creation not supported; SSO phase will enforce IdP-side policy

practice: Prohibit password reuse for a specified number of generations
notes: Defers to IdP (Google OAuth); local password management not in scope for v2; SSO phase enforces via IdP policy

practice: Allow temporary password use for system logons with an immediate change requirement
notes: Temporary credential lifecycle policy; defers to IdP; policy required for completeness

practice: Store and transmit only cryptographically-protected passwords
notes: Passwords not stored by the application (OAuth only); ApiKey and ScimToken use bcrypt hash+prefix; session cookie is httpOnly/Secure/SameSite

practice: Obscure feedback of authentication information during the authentication process
notes: OAuth flow does not echo credentials; no password input field in the application UI; session token not included in API response bodies

practice: Establish an operational incident-handling capability for organizational systems
notes: Incident response plan; IR procedures; contact lists; escalation paths — policy artifacts required by deploying organization

practice: Track; document; and report incidents to designated officials
notes: Incident tracking and reporting procedures; policy artifacts required by deploying organization

practice: Test the organizational incident response capability
notes: IR tabletop exercises and testing cadence; policy artifacts required by deploying organization

practice: Perform maintenance on organizational systems
notes: System maintenance procedures including scheduling and authorization; policy artifacts required by deploying organization

practice: Provide controls on the tools; techniques; mechanisms; and personnel for the performance of system maintenance
notes: Maintenance personnel authorization and tool control policy; policy artifacts required by deploying organization

practice: Ensure equipment removed for off-site maintenance is sanitized
notes: Physical equipment handling policy; not applicable to cloud-deployed software; policy required by deploying organization

practice: Check media containing diagnostic and test programs for malicious code before the media are used in organizational systems
notes: Media sanitization before use policy; policy required by deploying organization

practice: Require MFA for remote maintenance sessions
notes: Remote maintenance via SSH/admin API requires MFA; MFA not yet implemented; planned for SSO phase; this is a gov-go-live gate dependency (3.5.3 dependency)

practice: Supervise the maintenance activities of maintenance personnel without required access authorization
notes: Supervised maintenance procedures; policy required by deploying organization

practice: Protect (i.e.; physically control and securely store) system media containing CUI
notes: Physical media handling policy; cloud storage (MinIO/GovCloud) encrypted at rest; policy for physical media required by deploying organization

practice: Limit access to CUI on system media to authorized users
notes: Object storage access requires authenticated session + RBAC check; no direct unauthenticated storage access; ABAC requireAccess gates all data reads

practice: Sanitize or destroy system media before disposal or reuse
notes: Media sanitization/disposal policy; cloud provider (GovCloud/Assured Workloads) responsibility for physical media; policy required for deploying organization

practice: Mark media with necessary CUI markings and distribution limitations
notes: DataClassification markings (FOUO/CUI/handling instructions) persisted per entity; PDF/CSV/JSON exports include classification banners; markings.ts detectMarkings enforces banner presence

practice: Control access to media containing CUI and maintain accountability for media during transport
notes: Cloud provider (GCC-High / Assured Workloads) responsibility for physical media transport controls; inherited from platform (GCC-High physical media transport)

practice: Implement cryptographic mechanisms to protect the confidentiality of CUI during transit unless otherwise protected by alternative physical safeguards
notes: TLS via Caddy for all external traffic; internal service-to-service communication on isolated Docker network; MinIO access via HTTPS

practice: Control the use of removable media on system components
notes: Removable media policy; endpoint/infrastructure policy required by deploying organization

practice: Prohibit the use of portable storage devices when such devices have no identifiable owner
notes: Portable storage ownership policy; endpoint/infrastructure policy required by deploying organization

practice: Protect the confidentiality of backup CUI at storage locations
notes: MinIO object storage with encryption-at-rest planned; pgBackRest/Barman WAL archiving to in-boundary encrypted target planned for backup/DR phase; not yet implemented

practice: Screen individuals prior to authorizing access to organizational systems containing CUI
notes: Personnel screening / background check policy; HR/legal process required by deploying organization

practice: Ensure that CUI is protected during and after personnel actions such as terminations and transfers
notes: Separation/transfer procedures including account revocation; policy required by deploying organization

practice: Limit physical access to organizational systems to authorized individuals
notes: Physical access control to data center/hosting facility; inherited from cloud provider (GCC-High / Assured Workloads) for hosted deployment; policy required for on-prem deployments

practice: Protect and monitor the physical facility and support infrastructure for organizational systems
notes: Physical facility monitoring; cloud provider responsibility for hosted deployment; policy required for deploying organization

practice: Escort visitors and monitor visitor activity
notes: Physical visitor control policy; facility policy required by deploying organization

practice: Maintain audit logs of physical access
notes: Physical access log (badge readers/CCTV); cloud provider responsibility for hosted; policy required for deploying organization

practice: Control and manage physical access devices
notes: Key/badge management policy; cloud provider responsibility for hosted; policy required for deploying organization

practice: Enforce safeguarding measures for CUI at alternate work sites
notes: Telework/remote-work CUI handling policy; required by deploying organization

practice: Periodically assess the risk to organizational operations; assets; and individuals
notes: Risk assessment process and schedule; requires human-authored policy and a documented risk register; out of code scope

practice: Scan for vulnerabilities in organizational systems and applications periodically and when new vulnerabilities affecting those systems are identified
notes: Trivy SCA (fs scan) + OSV-Scanner on every CI run; Trivy image scan on GHCR digest; CodeQL SAST; Dependabot weekly; all gate outputs written to evidence/<sha>/

practice: Remediate vulnerabilities in accordance with risk assessments
notes: Critical CVEs are HARD-fail in security.yml (blocks promotion); High CVEs generate POA&M artifacts; formal remediation SLA policy not yet authored

practice: Periodically assess the security controls in organizational systems to determine if the controls are effective
notes: Formal security assessment process; C3PAO assessment requires this plus written policies; scheduled control-effectiveness reviews are policy artifacts

practice: Develop and implement plans of action designed to correct deficiencies and reduce or eliminate vulnerabilities in organizational systems
notes: security.yml High-CVE POA&M artifacts generated automatically; formal POA&M lifecycle management (tracking/closure/acceptance) not yet implemented; 5 open Critical image CVEs noted in POA&M

practice: Monitor security controls on an ongoing basis to ensure the continued effectiveness of the controls
notes: check:control-coverage CI gate monitors SSP completeness continuously; SAST/SCA gates run on every build; full CONMON (continuous monitoring) program not yet formalized

practice: Develop; document; and periodically update system security plans
notes: control-coverage.csv is the machine-readable SSP matrix (this file); SSP.md is the narrative document (co-located); check:control-coverage CI gate enforces freshness on every build

practice: Monitor; control; and protect communications at the external boundaries and key internal boundaries of organizational systems
notes: SSRF guard on all outbound URL references; Caddy reverse proxy enforces TLS at external boundary; internal services on isolated Docker network; gov tenants code-blocked from external MCP sidecars

practice: Employ architectural designs; software development techniques; and systems engineering principles that promote effective information security
notes: CUI-blind egress chokepoint architecture; single-path enforcement via ESLint + arch test; fail-closed defaults; chokepoint as the foundational security design principle

practice: Separate user functionality from system management functionality
notes: Platform-owner vs tenant-admin vs regular user separation; INTERNAL_ADMINS gating; management APIs require elevated permission bits

practice: Prevent unauthorized and unintended information transfer via shared system resources
notes: Egress chokepoint withholds CUI from external model; multi-tenant org-scoped queries prevent cross-tenant data access; orgId prefix on all query keys prevents cross-tenant cache bleed

practice: Implement subnetworks for publicly accessible system components
notes: Internal Docker network isolates cosmos/postgres/classifier; only Caddy has external port binding; cosmos service not directly internet-accessible

practice: Deny network communications traffic by default and allow network communications traffic by exception
notes: Docker internal network restricts inter-service communication; explicit ingress-only via Caddy; full network-policy default-deny (Kubernetes NetworkPolicy) deferred to Helm/K8s phase

practice: Prevent remote devices from simultaneously tunneling to the organizational system
notes: VPN/split-tunneling policy for remote users; infrastructure/endpoint policy required by deploying organization

practice: Implement cryptographic mechanisms to prevent unauthorized disclosure of CUI during transmission
notes: TLS enforced at Caddy for all external communication; HTTPS-only; HSTS headers enforced; internal Docker network for service-to-service

practice: Terminate network connections after a defined period of inactivity
notes: Session expiry enforced server-side; TCP keepalive and idle connection termination at Caddy not yet explicitly configured; planned

practice: Establish and manage cryptographic keys when cryptography is employed
notes: TLS certificates managed by Caddy (auto-ACME); application secrets managed via environment variable injection; formal KMS/key-rotation lifecycle not yet implemented; planned for secret-rotation phase

practice: Employ FIPS-validated cryptography when used to protect the confidentiality of CUI
notes: Node.js FIPS-mode and FIPS-validated modules not yet validated/tested; planned for gov-hardening phase; currently using standard TLS/AES implementations

practice: Prohibit remote activation of collaborative computing devices and provide indication of use
notes: Collaboration device policy (cameras/microphones); application does not control endpoints; policy required by deploying organization

practice: Control and monitor the use of mobile code
notes: CSP headers restrict script sources; client-side code is bundled and controlled; formal mobile-code policy and runtime monitoring not yet in place

practice: Control and monitor the use of VoIP technologies
notes: VoIP/unified communications policy; out of software scope; policy required by deploying organization

practice: Protect the authenticity of communications sessions
notes: Session tokens are cryptographically random UUIDs; httpOnly/Secure/SameSite cookie attributes; CSRF protection via SameSite; session bound to user identity

practice: Protect CUI at rest
notes: PostgreSQL data encryption-at-rest via host OS/cloud provider volume encryption; application-level AES-256-GCM envelope encryption for secrets (McpServer.env etc.) planned; MinIO at-rest encryption planned; full implementation pending secret-management phase

practice: Identify; report; and correct information and system flaws in a timely manner
notes: Trivy SCA + OSV-Scanner + Dependabot identify vulnerabilities; security.yml HARD-fails on Critical CVEs; High CVEs generate POA&M artifacts; flaw remediation tracked via Dependabot PRs

practice: Provide protection from malicious code at appropriate locations within organizational systems
notes: Trivy image scan (container + deps); OSV-Scanner (lockfile); gitleaks (secrets/malicious patterns); CodeQL SAST; all gate outputs in evidence/<sha>/

practice: Monitor system security alerts and advisories and take appropriate actions in response
notes: GitHub Dependabot generates security advisories; Trivy SCA on every build; formal alert triage/response SLA and ISAC subscription not yet documented; planned

practice: Update malicious code protection mechanisms when new releases are available
notes: Dependabot configured for npm + github-actions + docker ecosystems (weekly); automated PRs keep dependencies and action versions current; gate tools updated via Dependabot

practice: Perform periodic scans of organizational systems and real-time scans of files from external sources as files are downloaded; opened; or executed
notes: Trivy SCA filesystem scan + OSV lockfile scan on every CI run; image scan on every release build; gitleaks on every PR

practice: Monitor organizational systems including inbound and outbound communications traffic to detect attacks and indicators of compromise
notes: Egress chokepoint logs all model-bound decisions (EgressDecision table); SSRF guard monitors outbound webhook/connector traffic; full network-level IDS/IPS and SIEM integration not yet deployed; planned for observability phase

practice: Identify unauthorized use of organizational systems
notes: Audit log captures all user actions with timestamps and IP addresses; anomaly detection / behavioral analytics not yet implemented; unauthorized-use alerting planned for observability phase
